acceptanceCriteria: A concrete spec documents the unified session adapter contract, parser responsibilities, Atlas metadata inputs, native ID handling, unified ID handling, and compatibility behavior.
Stable fixtures cover claude-code, codex, pi, gemini, opencode, and at least one plugin-generated target.
Unit tests cover parser/listing behavior from stable native session fixtures and preserve existing adapter API compatibility.
Atlas projection tests prove runtime metadata inputs match SessionSemantics and PluginTarget graph records.
SessionManagerImpl and gateway session loading use the shared contract without duplicating session parsing behavior.
CLI and gateway e2e tests cover sessions list/read/show/full/export paths.
Docs explain the new contract, target matrix, troubleshooting, and plugin target extension path.
riskMitigations: Freeze representative fixtures before implementation to catch cross-adapter parser regressions.
Keep native session IDs and unified IDs separate and test both mapping directions.
Preserve sessionDir/listSessionFiles/parseSessionFile compatibility while introducing the shared registry.
Route gateway native-session fallback through the same shared contract as SDK/CLI session management.
Use Atlas metadata as tested runtime input rather than copying path metadata into a second hard-coded registry.
